logstash-logging-spring-boot-starter/pom.general_idea: Update the Java version property from 1.8 to 11 to target the new JDK baseline.
logstash-logging-spring-boot-starter/pom.why_exists: Without this change, the project continues to compile against Java 8 semantics, failing to enforce Java 11 compatibility or utilize new language features.

logstash-logging-spring-boot-starter/pom.general_idea: Replace javax.annotation-api with jakarta.annotation-api to align with the Jakarta EE namespace migration.
logstash-logging-spring-boot-starter/pom.why_exists: javax.annotation-api is deprecated/removed in newer ecosystems; using jakarta.annotation-api ensures compatibility with modern frameworks that have migrated to the jakarta namespace.

logstash-logging-spring-boot-starter/pom.general_idea: Switch maven-compiler-plugin configuration from separate source/target to the unified release flag.
logstash-logging-spring-boot-starter/pom.why_exists: The 'release' flag is the standard way to compile for a specific Java version in modern Maven setups, ensuring consistent module path and API usage for Java 11+.

pom.xml.general_idea: Set the Maven compiler release target to Java 11 to ensure bytecode compatibility with the new JDK version.
pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the project would compile to Java 8 bytecode by default, failing to utilize Java 11 features or potentially causing runtime issues if the target environment strictly enforces Java 11 class file versions.

reactive-logstash-logging-spring-boot-st.general_idea: Update the Java version property from 1.8 to 11 to target the new JDK baseline.
reactive-logstash-logging-spring-boot-st.why_exists: Without this change, the project continues to compile against Java 8 semantics, failing to enforce Java 11 compatibility or utilize new language features.

reactive-logstash-logging-spring-boot-st.general_idea: Replace javax.annotation-api with jakarta.annotation-api to align with the Jakarta EE namespace migration required for modern Spring Boot versions on Java 11.
reactive-logstash-logging-spring-boot-st.why_exists: The javax.annotation package was removed from the JDK in Java 11. While the old dependency provided it, modern frameworks (like Spring Boot 2.3+) expect the jakarta namespace or specific provided scopes to avoid conflicts and ensure compatibility with the new module system.

reactive-logstash-logging-spring-boot-st.general_idea: Switch maven-compiler-plugin configuration from separate source/target to the unified release flag for Java 9+ compilation.
reactive-logstash-logging-spring-boot-st.why_exists: The 'release' flag is the standard way to compile for Java 9 and later, ensuring that the code only uses APIs available in the target version. Using source/target with Java 11 can lead to subtle API availability issues if not carefully managed, and 'release' is the recommended approach.
